Show that the following arguments are valid by giving a proof of the conclusion
from the premises:

a. $\some{A}{B}\proves\some{B}{A}$

b. $\all{A}{B}, \no{B}{C}, \some{C}{D}\proves \no{A}{C}$

c. $\all{A}{B}, \some{A}{A}\proves\some{B}{A}$

d. $\no{A}{B}, \some{B}{A}\proves\somenot{D}{C}$

e. $\some{A}{B}, \no{B}{C}\proves\somenot{A}{C}$

f. $\somenot{A}{B}, \all{C}{B}\proves\somenot{A}{C}$

g. $\no{A}{B}, \some{C}{A}, \all{D}{B}\proves\somenot{C}{D}$
